chunks.title: Reshaping
chunks.text: next we are going to discuss about important operations on numpy arrays

chunks.title: Reshaping
chunks.text: the first and basic operation is of reshaping an array

chunks.title: Reshaping
chunks.text: let's suppose we have an array with dimensions 2x3

chunks.title: Reshaping
chunks.text: means it has 2 rows and 3 columns

chunks.title: Reshaping
chunks.text: we can reshape this array if we want

chunks.title: Reshaping
chunks.text: if we have elements like this

chunks.title: Reshaping
chunks.text: we can change the dimensions or change the shape of this array to 3x2

chunks.title: Reshaping
chunks.text: how will we convert this?

chunks.title: Reshaping
chunks.text: it basically converts to this model

chunks.title: Reshaping
chunks.text: where we have only 2 columns and 3 rows

chunks.title: Reshaping
chunks.text: and our elements are divided like this

chunks.title: Reshaping
chunks.text: so in this way we can reshape our array

chunks.title: Reshaping
chunks.text: let's try it out

chunks.title: Reshaping
chunks.text: here we are actually going to talk about operations on arrays

chunks.title: Reshaping
chunks.text: when we are talking about reshaping

chunks.title: Reshaping
chunks.text: reshaping means let's create a normal array first

chunks.title: Reshaping
chunks.text: we can create an array of 2x3 size from np.array

chunks.title: Reshaping
chunks.text: we have 1,2,3 and 4,5,6

chunks.title: Reshaping
chunks.text: let's print the array along with the array shape

chunks.title: Reshaping
chunks.text: it looks something like this

chunks.title: Reshaping
chunks.text: what we will do is reshape it

chunks.title: Reshaping
chunks.text: to reshape the array we can actually write array.reshape

chunks.title: Reshaping
chunks.text: and we can pass our new dimensions in it

chunks.title: Reshaping
chunks.text: which will be 3,2

chunks.title: Reshaping
chunks.text: and we can print the reshaped array

chunks.title: Reshaping
chunks.text: and it will look something like this

chunks.title: Reshaping
chunks.text: it is exactly the same as we imagined

chunks.title: Reshaping
chunks.text: and now its dimensions have changed to 3x2

chunks.title: Reshaping
chunks.text: reshaping can only be done when we have a compatible number of elements

chunks.title: Reshaping
chunks.text: it is not that we can reshape 2x3 to 2x5 or 3x5

chunks.title: Reshaping
chunks.text: we should have equal number of elements in both the arrays

chunks.title: Reshaping
chunks.text: both the arrays should have equal number of elements

chunks.title: Reshaping
chunks.text: both the arrays should have equal number of elements

chunks.title: Reshaping
chunks.text: only then reshaping can be done

chunks.title: Reshaping
chunks.text: along with reshaping we can also flatten the arrays

chunks.title: Reshaping
chunks.text: flatten means converting it into a 1D array

chunks.title: Reshaping
chunks.text: to create a flattened array i can do array.flatten

chunks.title: Reshaping
chunks.text: so this basically converts our 2D array to a 1D array

chunks.title: Reshaping
chunks.text: for this also we can print

chunks.title: Reshaping
chunks.text: our array, what will come finally

chunks.title: Reshaping
chunks.text: and the shape of our flattened array

chunks.title: Reshaping
chunks.text: so this is what the array will be

chunks.title: Reshaping
chunks.text: and this is what the shape will be

chunks.title: Reshaping
chunks.text: which is one dimensional array of 6 elements

chunks.title: Reshaping
chunks.text: so in this way we perform the basic operation which is reshaping

text: next we are going to discuss about important operations on numpy arrays the first and basic operation is of reshaping an array let's suppose we have an array with dimensions 2x3 means it has 2 rows and 3 columns we can reshape this array if we want if we have elements like this we can change the dimensions or change the shape of this array to 3x2 how will we convert this? it basically converts to this model where we have only 2 columns and 3 rows and our elements are divided like this so in this way we can reshape our array let's try it out here we are actually going to talk about operations on arrays when we are talking about reshaping reshaping means let's create a normal array first we can create an array of 2x3 size from np.array we have 1,2,3 and 4,5,6 let's print the array along with the array shape it looks something like this what we will do is reshape it to reshape the array we can actually write array.reshape and we can pass our new dimensions in it which will be 3,2 and we can print the reshaped array and it will look something like this it is exactly the same as we imagined and now its dimensions have changed to 3x2 reshaping can only be done when we have a compatible number of elements it is not that we can reshape 2x3 to 2x5 or 3x5 we should have equal number of elements in both the arrays both the arrays should have equal number of elements both the arrays should have equal number of elements only then reshaping can be done along with reshaping we can also flatten the arrays flatten means converting it into a 1D array to create a flattened array i can do array.flatten so this basically converts our 2D array to a 1D array for this also we can print our array, what will come finally and the shape of our flattened array so this is what the array will be and this is what the shape will be which is one dimensional array of 6 elements so in this way we perform the basic operation which is reshaping
